title: centaurea limbata subsp. limbata
intro: Centaurea limbata subsp. limbata, also known as a type of thistle, is a flowering plant belonging to the aster family (Asteraceae) and native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its rich content of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Centaurea limbata subsp. limbata can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal usage.
